nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Copplestone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestone was born in 1244, in Copplestone, Devon, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Richard de Coplestone. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestone est née en 1244, à Copplestone, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Richard de Coplestone. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestone wurde 1244 in Copplestone, Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Richard de Coplestone mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestone è nata il nel 1244 nel Copplestone, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Richard de Coplestone. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Copplestone, Devon, England, United King: イギリスイギリスデボンコップレストーン
ja.Copplestone, Devon, England: イギリスイギリスデボンコップレストーン
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestoneは1244年にイギリスイギリスデボンコップレストーンで生まれた。彼女はRichard de Coplestoneとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestone(은)는 1244년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 코플레스톤에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Richard de Coplestone(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestone nasceu em 1244, em Copplestone, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Richard de Coplestone. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestone родилась в 1244 г. в местности копплестоун, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Richard de Coplestone. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard de Copplestone nació en 1244, en Copplestone, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Richard de Coplestone. Murió en su pueblo.
